Building FreeCell Solitaire in JavaScript: The Tricky Bits Nobody Warns You About

Lately I've been poking at how to build a FreeCell-style Solitaire game, and there are a few small tricky bits worth jotting down. So this post is basically a collection of directions and hints I picked up along the way.

For random shuffling, the idea is pretty much the same as generating random numbers. I actually built a little thing before about

Before we get to random shuffling, we need to understand one thing first: generating random numbers.
A deck of cards has 54 cards, but here I'm only going to generate 52, because the other 2 are jokers and they don't go into Solitaire.

Of course this is just a simple example. If you really want to build Solitaire, there are a few key conditions still missing:
Check the total count across all arrays (e.g. the total card count can't go over or under 52)
The same number can't appear more than 4 times (e.g. the number 8 shows up more than 4 times)

I've been digging into how to build FreeCell-style Solitaire in JavaScript, and there are a bunch of small tricky bits worth writing down. This post collects the directions and hints I figured out — drag and drop (both jQuery UI and vanilla JS), generating random numbers, shuffling cards into columns, and the extra conditions a real Solitaire game needs.
